title: The Lost Little Koala
blurb: A baby koala gets lost in a storm and must be brave to find her way home.
paragraphs: Kira was a small koala who lived high in a tall gum tree. One windy afternoon, dark clouds rolled across the sky. Kira held on tight, but a strong gust shook the branch and she tumbled down, down, down into the bushes below.
When the rain stopped, Kira looked around. Every tree looked the same. She could not see her mother anywhere. "Stay calm," she told herself. "I can do this."
Kira sniffed the air. She remembered that her tree smelled of sweet eucalyptus leaves. Slowly, she followed the smell, climbing over wet logs and around muddy puddles.
After a long walk, Kira heard a soft call. "Kira! Kira!" It was her mother! Kira scrambled up the trunk and hugged her tight. She had been brave, and she had found her way home.
That night, Kira fell asleep listening to the wind. She was not scared anymore. She knew she was stronger than she thought.
wonder: How do you think Kira felt when she could not see her mother?
What clever idea helped Kira find her way home?

title: Bilby and the Big Race
blurb: A little bilby learns that trying your best matters more than winning.
paragraphs: Every year, the desert animals held a big race across the red sand. This year, Benny the bilby decided to join. His friends giggled. "Your legs are too short!" they said.
Benny did not mind. He trained every morning, running past the spiky spinifex grass until his legs grew strong.
On race day, the emu shot ahead, kicking up dust. Benny ran as fast as he could, but he came in last. He was puffed and sweaty.
Then Benny noticed a young echidna stuck in a hole near the finish line. While the others celebrated, Benny stopped and gently helped the echidna out.
"You did not win the race," said the echidna, "but you have the biggest heart of all." Benny smiled. That felt even better than first place.
wonder: Why did Benny's friends think he could not race?
What did Benny do that showed he was kind?

title: The Kangaroo Who Couldn't Hop
blurb: Joey the kangaroo wants to hop like the others, but his legs just won't bounce.
paragraphs: Joey was a young kangaroo with a big problem. All the other joeys could bounce high across the paddock, but every time Joey tried, he just flopped over.
"Maybe I will never hop," Joey sighed. His mum nuzzled him. "Keep trying, little one. Strong legs take time."
Each day, Joey practised. He wobbled. He fell. He bumped his tail. But slowly, his back legs grew stronger and springier.
One sunny morning, Joey crouched low, swished his tail, and pushed hard. Boing! He flew into the air and landed neatly on the grass.
"I did it!" Joey shouted. He hopped all the way home, the happiest kangaroo in the whole paddock.
wonder: What did Joey's mum tell him when he felt sad?
How did practising every day help Joey?

title: Stripes the Curious Quokka
blurb: A quokka on a sunny island discovers what is hiding behind the big rock.
paragraphs: On a warm island near the sea lived a quokka named Stripes. Stripes loved to explore. He always wanted to know what was around the next corner.
One day, Stripes spotted a huge rock he had never been behind. "I wonder what is back there," he whispered.
He hopped over driftwood and squeezed past prickly bushes. Behind the rock he found a hidden pool of clear blue water, sparkling in the sun.
Tiny crabs danced on the sand, and bright fish flashed below the surface. Stripes had found a secret place all his own.
From then on, Stripes visited the pool every day. Being curious had led him to something wonderful.
wonder: What does the word 'curious' mean?
What did Stripes find behind the big rock?

title: The Great Beach Cleanup
blurb: When Maya finds rubbish on her favourite beach, she gathers her friends to help.
paragraphs: Maya loved going to the beach near her home in Melbourne. She loved the salty air, the squawking seagulls, and the cool waves washing over her toes.
But one Saturday, Maya saw something that made her frown. The sand was covered in plastic bottles, bags, and bits of old rope. A little turtle was tangled in a piece of net.
Maya carefully freed the turtle and watched it paddle safely back into the sea. "This beach needs help," she said.
The next weekend, Maya brought her whole class. They wore gloves and carried bags, picking up every piece of rubbish they could find. By lunchtime, the beach was clean again.
An old fisherman walked by and smiled. "You children have done a wonderful thing," he said. Maya felt proud. Looking after nature was everybody's job, and it started with her.
wonder: Why was Maya upset when she got to the beach?
What can you do to help look after the place where you live?

title: Milo's First Day at School
blurb: Milo has butterflies in his tummy on his first day at a new school.
paragraphs: Milo's family had just moved to a new suburb. On Monday morning, he had to start at a brand new school. His tummy felt full of fluttering butterflies.
"What if I have no friends?" he asked. His dad gave him a hug. "Just be yourself, Milo. Smile and say hello."
At school, Milo sat quietly in the corner. Then a girl named Priya walked over. "Hi! Do you want to play handball with us?" she asked.
Milo grinned and nodded. Soon he was laughing and racing around the playground with his new friends.
When his dad picked him up, Milo couldn't stop talking. "I love my new school!" he said. The butterflies were all gone.
wonder: Why did Milo feel nervous about his first day?
How did Priya help Milo feel better?

title: The Magic Boomerang
blurb: Jack finds an old boomerang that always returns — even from impossible places.
paragraphs: While exploring his grandfather's shed, Jack found a beautiful old boomerang covered in painted patterns. "Take it outside and give it a throw," his grandfather said with a wink.
Jack went to the open field behind the house. He swung his arm and let the boomerang fly. It curved through the air in a wide arc and came spinning right back into his hand.
"Wow!" said Jack. He threw it again, harder this time. The boomerang sailed over the trees, looped around a hill, and still found its way home.
No matter how far Jack threw it, the boomerang always returned. It was as if it could never get lost.

That evening, his grandfather explained that the boomerang was very old and had been made with great care and skill long ago. "Some things," he said, "are made to always come back to you." Jack held it close, feeling lucky to be its keeper.
wonder: What was special about the boomerang Jack found?
Why do you think the boomerang was important to Jack's grandfather?

title: The Night the Stars Fell
blurb: Two friends camp in the backyard and witness an amazing shower of shooting stars.
paragraphs: Ava and Sam set up a tent in the backyard for a sleepover under the sky. They lay on their backs and stared up into the darkness.
"Look!" gasped Ava. A bright streak of light shot across the sky. Then another, and another. The stars seemed to be falling!
"They're shooting stars," Sam whispered. "Dad said it's called a meteor shower. Quick, make a wish!" They both squeezed their eyes shut and wished hard.
For an hour, they counted dozens of glowing streaks. The sky put on a show just for them.
When they finally fell asleep, they were still smiling. It was a night they would remember forever — the night the stars fell.
wonder: What was really happening when the stars seemed to fall?
If you saw a shooting star, what would you wish for?

title: Ruby and the Rainbow Lorikeet
blurb: Ruby helps an injured lorikeet and gains a colourful little friend.
paragraphs: Ruby was playing in her garden when she heard a sad little squawk. Under the bottlebrush tree sat a rainbow lorikeet with a hurt wing.
Gently, Ruby scooped the bird into a soft towel. "Don't worry," she said. "I'll look after you."
Ruby and her mum took the lorikeet to the vet. The vet wrapped its wing and gave Ruby some special food to feed it.
Every day, Ruby cared for the bird until its wing was strong again. Then came the day to let it go.
Ruby opened her hands and the lorikeet flew up into the blue sky. But every morning after that, it returned to her window to say hello. Ruby had made a friend for life.
wonder: What was wrong with the lorikeet when Ruby found it?
How did Ruby show that she was a caring person?

title: The Cubby House Club
blurb: Four friends build the best cubby house ever — but only by working together.
paragraphs: Tom, Lily, Hassan, and Grace wanted to build a cubby house in the big backyard. Each of them had a different idea, and at first they all argued.
"It should have a flag!" said Tom. "No, a secret door!" said Lily. Soon nobody was building anything at all.
Then Grace had a thought. "Why don't we use ALL our ideas?" she said. "Tom can make the flag. Lily can make the door. Hassan is good at hammering, and I'll draw the plan."
With everyone helping, the cubby house slowly came together. By sunset, it had a flag, a secret door, sturdy walls, and even a little window.
The four friends crawled inside and grinned. "This is the best cubby in the world," said Hassan. "And we built it as a team."
wonder: Why couldn't the friends build anything at the start?
How did working as a team help them succeed?

title: The Brave Little Firetruck
blurb: Pip is the smallest firetruck in the station, but one day she saves the day.
paragraphs: Pip was the smallest firetruck in the whole fire station. The big trucks zoomed off to fight fires, but Pip was usually left behind.
"You're too little," the big trucks said. Pip felt sad, but she kept her engine shiny and her hose ready, just in case.
One day, a fire started in a narrow laneway. The big trucks were too wide to fit through the tiny gap.
"This is a job for Pip!" said the fire chief. Pip squeezed through the narrow lane, aimed her hose, and sprayed water until the fire was out.
Everyone cheered. From that day on, nobody ever called Pip 'too little' again. Being small had made her just the right size for the job.
wonder: Why was Pip usually left behind at the station?
How did being small help Pip save the day?

title: Grandpa's Veggie Patch
blurb: Ella learns that good things grow for those who are patient.
paragraphs: Every Sunday, Ella helped her grandpa in his veggie patch. They planted tiny carrot and tomato seeds in the soft brown soil.
"When can we eat them?" asked Ella the very next day. Grandpa chuckled. "Not yet, little one. Plants need time, water, and sunshine."
Ella watered the seeds every day. She waited and waited. Some days she thought nothing was happening at all.
Then one morning, tiny green shoots poked up through the dirt. Week by week they grew taller, until bright tomatoes and crunchy carrots were ready to pick.
Ella bit into a sweet tomato and grinned. "It was worth the wait," she said. Grandpa nodded. "The best things often are."
wonder: What three things did Grandpa say plants need to grow?
What does it mean to be patient?

title: The Mystery of the Missing Cricket Ball
blurb: When the cricket ball vanishes, young detective Sam follows the clues.
paragraphs: It was a hot summer day and the kids were playing backyard cricket. Sam hit the ball with a mighty whack — and it disappeared! Everyone searched, but the ball was nowhere to be found.
Sam decided to become a detective. "Every mystery has clues," he said. He looked at the muddy ground and spotted some paw prints leading away from the pitch.
He followed the prints across the lawn, under the trampoline, and behind the shed. The prints stopped at a doghouse.
Inside, curled up fast asleep, was Rusty the dog — with the cricket ball tucked safely between his paws!
Everyone laughed. Rusty had thought it was a brand new toy. Sam the detective had solved the case, and Rusty got his very own ball to keep.
wonder: What clue helped Sam find the missing ball?
Why do you think Rusty took the cricket ball?

title: Echidna's Spiky Problem
blurb: Eddie the echidna wishes he wasn't so spiky — until his spikes save him.
paragraphs: Eddie the echidna was covered in sharp, spiky spines. He wished he had soft fur like the other animals. "My spikes make it hard to hug my friends," he sighed.
One afternoon, a hungry goanna crept towards Eddie, licking its lips. Eddie was scared!
Quick as a flash, Eddie curled up into a tight, prickly ball. The goanna tried to grab him but only got a nose full of sharp spikes. "Ouch!" it cried, and ran away.
Eddie slowly uncurled. His spikes had kept him safe after all.
From that day, Eddie felt proud of his spines. "They might be prickly," he said, "but they are exactly what makes me, me."
wonder: Why did Eddie wish he was different at the start?
How did Eddie's spikes turn out to be a good thing?

title: A Trip to the Great Barrier Reef
blurb: Noah goes snorkelling and discovers a magical underwater world.
paragraphs: Noah's family went on holiday to Queensland to visit the Great Barrier Reef. Noah put on his mask and snorkel and floated face-down in the warm, clear water.
Below him stretched a world of colour. Coral grew in shapes like brains, fans, and antlers. Fish in every colour darted in and out of the reef.
A green sea turtle glided past, flapping its flippers slowly like wings. Noah held his breath in wonder.
His mum pointed to a clownfish hiding in a wavy sea anemone, just like in the movies. Noah waved at it, and it peeked back at him.
Back on the boat, Noah couldn't stop smiling. "The reef is like a city under the sea," he said. "We have to look after it."
wonder: Name two animals Noah saw under the water.
Why did Noah say we have to look after the reef?

title: The Dancing Cockatoo
blurb: Coco the cockatoo loves to dance, even when others think it's silly.
paragraphs: Coco was a white cockatoo with a bright yellow crest. Whenever music played, Coco could not help but bob her head and dance.
The other birds in the park thought it was silly. "Birds are supposed to sing, not dance," they squawked.
But Coco did not stop. She raised her crest, lifted her feet, and swayed to the beat of the buskers playing nearby.
Soon, a crowd of people gathered, clapping and smiling at the dancing cockatoo. They threw coins into the busker's hat and cheered for Coco.
The other birds watched in surprise. Maybe being different wasn't so silly after all. Coco just grinned and kept on dancing.
wonder: Why did the other birds think Coco was silly?
What happened when Coco kept dancing anyway?

title: Lily and the Lighthouse
blurb: In a wild storm, Lily helps her grandmother keep the lighthouse shining.
paragraphs: Lily was staying with her grandmother, who looked after an old lighthouse on a rocky point by the sea. At night, the great light spun round and round, warning ships to stay away from the rocks.
One evening, a fierce storm rolled in. Rain lashed the windows and waves crashed against the cliffs. Then, with a flicker, the lighthouse lamp went out!
"The bulb has blown," said Grandma. "A ship is out there in the dark. We must fix it, quickly!"
Lily climbed the winding stairs with a new bulb while Grandma held the torch. Together they fitted it, and the great light blazed back to life.
Far out at sea, a ship's captain spotted the beam just in time and steered safely past the rocks. Lily had helped save the day, high up in the lighthouse.
wonder: What is a lighthouse used for?
How did Lily and her grandmother work together in the storm?

title: The Footy Final
blurb: Zoe's team is losing the grand final, but the final whistle teaches her something.
paragraphs: Zoe's footy team, the Magpies, had made it all the way to the grand final. The whole school came to watch. Zoe's tummy flipped with excitement and nerves.
The other team, the Tigers, were fast and strong. By the last quarter, the Magpies were behind by two goals. Zoe ran as hard as she could, her cheeks red and her heart pounding.
In the final minute, Zoe kicked a brilliant goal. The crowd roared! But when the siren sounded, the Tigers had still won by a single point.
Zoe felt like crying. Then the Tigers' captain walked over and shook her hand. "You played a great game," she said. "That was the best goal all day."
Zoe smiled and shook hands with every player. They had lost the match, but Zoe learned that how you play matters just as much as whether you win.
wonder: How did the Tigers' captain show good sportsmanship?
What did Zoe learn from losing the grand final?

title: The Friendly Dragon of Dandenong
blurb: The townsfolk are afraid of the dragon on the mountain — until they meet him.
paragraphs: High in the misty Dandenong hills lived a dragon named Doug. The people in the town below were terrified of him. "A dragon must be dangerous!" they whispered.
But Doug was actually very gentle. He spent his days reading books, growing ferns, and feeling rather lonely.
One cold winter, the town ran out of firewood and the people began to shiver. A brave girl named Mei climbed the mountain to ask the dragon for help.
To her surprise, Doug smiled kindly. With one warm, gentle breath, he lit the town's lanterns and fireplaces, keeping everyone cosy and warm.
After that, the townsfolk visited Doug often. They had learned an important lesson: you should never judge someone before you get to know them.
wonder: Why were the townsfolk afraid of Doug at first?
What lesson did the town learn about the dragon?

title: Possum on the Roof
blurb: A noisy possum keeps the family awake, so they find a clever, kind solution.
paragraphs: Thump, thump, thump! Every night, a brushtail possum ran across the roof of Oliver's house. The whole family was tired from the noise.
"We can't hurt the possum," said Mum. "It was here before us. We need a clever idea."
Oliver had a thought. "What if we build the possum its own home in the garden?" The family agreed to try.
Together they built a cosy wooden possum box and fixed it high in a tree. They placed a piece of apple inside as a welcome gift.
That night, the possum found its new home and snuggled in. The roof was finally quiet, and the possum had a safe place to sleep. Everyone was happy.
wonder: Why didn't the family want to hurt the possum?
What clever solution did Oliver come up with?
